issue: The defensive rejection handler calls labelFor(items[index], index) again; if the original settlement failure was caused by a throwing label accessor, this catch throws too, leaving the ignored .finally() chain to emit an unhandled rejection while the pool still resolves with an unset result.
fix: Compute a safe fallback label without re-reading the item, or wrap fallback result construction in its own try/catch and terminate the chain.

issue: spawnSync blocks the Node event loop inside the pool worker, so the capped pool cannot actually keep multiple lineage subprocesses in flight. This makes CLI fan-out effectively serial despite config.fanout.concurrency.
fix: Replace spawnSync with async spawn/promise settlement, preserving cwd/env/input/timeout/log capture.

issue: The spawned lineage receives a custom prompt to read the skill and run phases, rather than invoking the existing deep-loop command/YAML with a synthesized per-lineage config. That diverges from the packet requirement that each lineage runs the existing command verbatim, and leaves command parsing, validation, session binding, and max-iteration behavior to model interpretation.
fix: Launch the existing deep-loop command path per lineage with explicit config.executor, artifact-dir override, session_id, and max-iteration bindings.

issue: lineage.iterations is used only to size the subprocess timeout, not forwarded into the loop as max_iterations. A configured per-lineage iteration cap can therefore change timeout behavior without changing the actual loop cap.
fix: Pass lineage.iterations ?? default into the per-lineage command/config as max_iterations.

issue: The research branch reads deep-research-findings-registry.json, but the deep-research reducer writes findings-registry.json, so fan-out research can merge stale or empty registries and drop reducer-produced findings.
fix: Align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have fanout-merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback.

issue: Research dedup keys on finding.id, whose reducer-produced value is lineage-local, so identical findings from different lineages do not dedup and attribution is split.
fix: Dedup by contentHash when present, otherwise normalized text, and merge sources plus lineage attribution.

issue: Review dedup keys on lineage-local findingId/title instead of the reducer content-hash contract, causing unrelated local IDs to collide and same findings with different local IDs to duplicate.
fix: Use contentHash primary and file:line plus normalized title fallback, preserving highest severity while merging dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineages.

issue: Malformed registry JSON is swallowed as null and later skipped, so a lineage with an active P0 can be omitted from the merged review verdict.
fix: Return a distinct parse-failure state and fail closed on malformed lineage registries instead of counting them as skipped.

issue: Salvage writes unpadded iteration filenames, while the command surface expects iteration-{NNN}.md, so recovered files are written to paths downstream validation and merge do not consume.
fix: Format iteration ids with the shared three-digit NNN resolver or pass the resolved iteration path into salvage.

issue: Recovered stdout is written and counted as salvaged without reusing validateIterationOutputs, skipping canonical JSONL, delta, executor provenance, and v2 schema checks after recovery.
fix: Run post-dispatch validation before and after salvage; increment salvaged only after the recovered artifact validates.

issue: Recovered text is extracted once from whole-lineage stdout and reused for every missing iteration, so multiple missing files can receive the same transcript instead of per-iteration output.
fix: Recover from per-iteration logs or split stdout by iteration before writing each iteration artifact.

issue: JSON text parts and raw stdout are accepted/truncated without a substantive-content or truncation check, allowing whitespace-only or partial markdown to become a non-empty successful artifact.
fix: Require trimmed substantive content, surface truncation as degraded/failure, and make validation reject salvage-failure or truncated placeholders.

issue: fanoutConfigSchema only models the fanout block and strips extra top-level keys, so a raw config carrying both executor and fanout data is accepted instead of failing the both-present conflict contract.
fix: Add an explicit root-level executor-vs-fanout parser/guard, or make this parser strict and reject executor when present.

issue: parseFanoutConfig parses each lineage through lineageExecutorSchema before delegating to parseExecutorConfig, so legacy type is stripped and never reaches the normalizer/conflict detector; type:"cli-codex" without kind can become default native.
fix: Preprocess each raw lineage with the same legacy normalization before schema parsing, or make the lineage schema accept and reconcile type before stripping unknown keys.

issue: The default research config stores the executor as executor.kind, but the auto workflow branches on executor.type. With no fan-out flags, executor.type is absent, so the native single-executor branch is not selected and the default path cannot remain byte-identical.
fix: Branch and skip on config.executor.kind, or normalize legacy type to kind before workflow predicates run.

issue: The confirm research workflow branches on absent executor.type while the config template defaults to executor.kind. A no-fan-out default native run will miss the if_native branch, changing or blocking the single-executor output path.
fix: Branch and skip on config.executor.kind, or normalize legacy type to kind before workflow predicates run.

issue: initDb() still has a destructive migration path that drops coverage tables when an existing schema_version is older than SCHEMA_VERSION, violating the zero-destructive-migration expectation for session_id-based isolation.
fix: Replace drop-and-recreate with additive migration or fail-closed behavior; do not require a schema bump for fan-out session_id isolation.

issue: createSignalSnapshot() uses getStats(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType), which is not session-scoped, so direct library snapshots can aggregate rows from sibling lineages in the shared SQLite.
fix: Compute snapshot counts from getNodes(ns)/getEdges(ns), or add and pass sessionId through getStats.

issue: Native fan-out dispatch references fanout_run_id in the session_id template, but the deep YAMLs do not bind fanout_run_id, risking unresolved/literal session ids and cross-run collisions for native lineages.
fix: Bind fanout_run_id once before native dispatch, or reuse fanout-run.cjs' concrete run-id/session-id generation for native lineages.

issue: The :auto setup write shape still names only config.executor.*, while the same entrypoint later routes fan-out flags to config.fanout; the sibling research entrypoint has the same gap at .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:60.
fix: Document and bind an explicit XOR shape: either config.executor or config.fanout plus config.fanout_json.

issue: The command surface accepts repeatable --executor groups and --executors=<json> but does not define a fail-fast rule or precedence when both are present; research mirrors this at .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:136.
fix: Reject mixed --executor and --executors with a clear validation error before config write.

issue: --concurrency=N is parsed into config.fanout.concurrency, but 0-1 executor invocations stay on the single-executor path, so --concurrency without fan-out can silently do nothing; research mirrors this at .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:137.
fix: Reject --concurrency unless fan-out mode is selected, or make it explicitly select fan-out.

issue: parseFanoutConfig validates only the fan-out block, so the runtime never enforces the documented config.executor XOR config.fanout invariant if command setup writes both.
fix: Add a top-level loop dispatch config validator that rejects both-present configs and call it before YAML dispatch or fanout execution.

issue: step_fanout_spawn only skips when config.fanout is absent, so a spawned lineage that still has config.fanout can recursively fan out instead of running only its own lineage.
fix: Extend the guard to skip when config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir is present.

issue: step_fanout_spawn only skips when config.fanout is absent, so a spawned lineage that still has config.fanout can recursively fan out instead of running only its own lineage.
fix: Extend the guard to skip when config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir is present.

issue: step_fanout_spawn only skips when config.fanout is absent, so a spawned lineage that still has config.fanout can recursively fan out instead of running only its own lineage.
fix: Extend the guard to skip when config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir is present.

issue: step_fanout_spawn only skips when config.fanout is absent, so a spawned lineage that still has config.fanout can recursively fan out instead of running only its own lineage.
fix: Extend the guard to skip when config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir is present.

issue: The docs advertise CLI fan-out as parallel/capped, but the runner calls blocking spawnSync inside the pool worker, so the event loop cannot admit multiple subprocesses concurrently.
fix: Replace spawnSync with async spawn/execFile promise handling, or change the docs to describe CLI fan-out as sequential.

issue: The research and review command docs still say validation fails for reserved-but-unwired executor kinds, but cli-gemini and cli-claude-code are wired in executor-config and fanout-run.
fix: Remove the reserved/unwired wording and describe the current per-executor validation rules.

issue: The comment points to a stale spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml file for wave steps, while the live auto YAML keeps wave orchestration reference-only and excluded from workflow steps.
fix: Point to deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml reference-only appendix and state that intra-lineage wave orchestration is deferred.

issue: The resource domain table still says scripts/*.cjs has only four entry points, despite the shipped fanout scripts documented later and present on disk.
fix: Update the resource-domain and routing text to include fanout-pool, fanout-run, fanout-salvage, and fanout-merge.

issue: spawnSync runs inside the pool worker, so each async worker blocks the event loop before the next lineage can be admitted; the fan-out pool tests use a cooperative async worker and do not prove production CLI lineages overlap.
fix: Replace this with async spawn/execFile and add a fanout-run overlap test with two sleeping stubs.

issue: The direct spawnSync path bypasses the audited async supervisor's detached process-group timeout handling, so a timed-out lineage can leave child/grandchild processes behind.
fix: Route fanout CLI execution through the existing audited async runner or duplicate its detached process-group kill/escalation behavior.

issue: config.fanout_json is interpolated into a shell command inside single quotes before fanout-run.cjs can validate it; executor model strings are not quote-restricted, so a single quote in JSON can break out into shell syntax.
fix: Pass fanout config via a temp JSON file, base64/stdin, or an argv-safe Node wrapper instead of shell interpolation.

issue: config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir is bound directly as artifact_dir with no normalization or containment check, so a malformed override can escape the intended packet lineage tree.
fix: Resolve the override and reject any path outside the expected artifact_dir/lineages/label root.

issue: next_safe_action still points to Phase 002 work even though the child spec says Phase 002/003 are done and packet 123 is shipped.
fix: Update the implementation-summary continuity block to the current resume action, then regenerate metadata.

issue: The implementation summary says the child still fails validate.sh --strict, contradicting its completion_pct: 100 completion state and packet-level completion claim.
fix: Add the missing validation docs/template anchors and evidence, or downgrade the completion state.

issue: next_safe_action still points to Phase 003 implementation work while the child spec says Phase 003 is done and the packet is shipped.
fix: Refresh the implementation-summary continuity block to match the current shipped state.

issue: Graph metadata says status planned while the spec frontmatter claims Phase 3 complete with completion_pct: 100; implementation-summary.md is also absent from the inspected inventory.
fix: Add the missing implementation summary and regenerate graph metadata to complete.

issue: Graph metadata says status planned while the spec frontmatter claims Phase 4 complete with completion_pct: 100; implementation-summary.md is also absent from the inspected inventory.
fix: Add the missing implementation summary and regenerate graph metadata to complete.

issue: Graph metadata says status planned while the spec frontmatter claims Phase 5 complete with completion_pct: 100; implementation-summary.md is also absent from the inspected inventory.
fix: Add the missing implementation summary and regenerate graph metadata to complete.

issue: Graph metadata says status planned while the spec frontmatter claims Phase 6 complete with completion_pct: 100, undercutting the final packet-complete continuity claim.
fix: Add the missing implementation summary and regenerate graph metadata to complete.

issue: The iterationPlan schema allows bands expressed by count, but the real fan-out API already uses count for replica expansion while per-lineage iteration limits live in iterations. A naive 9+1 desugar could create nine replicas instead of one lineage capped at nine iterations.
fix: Define band cardinality as iterations or explicitly map band count/range length to lineage.iterations, never lineage.count.

issue: The ordered-band design says sequencing is enforced inside the existing pool driver, but native lineages bypass fanout-run.cjs and are dispatched by separate YAML branches. Mixed native/CLI schedules cannot be ordered by a pool-driver-only dependsOn/order extension.
fix: Put sequencing in a coordinator above both CLI and native branches, or route native lineages through the same scheduler.

issue: The spec treats minimax and bare codex/claude/opencode/gemini/devin as registered executor kinds, but the real executor enum uses native and cli-* kinds only. The motivating MiniMax schedule is not desugarable without either a supported executor mapping or a new executor kind, which this line scopes out.
fix: Use canonical executor kinds and provider-qualified models, or explicitly bring a minimax executor addition into scope.

issue: config.fanout_json is interpolated into a shell command inside single quotes, so a value containing an apostrophe can break out before fanout-run.cjs validation runs; the same pattern exists in the other fanout YAML command blocks.
fix: Pass fanout config via stdin/temp file or a shell-escaping helper, not raw interpolation.

issue: Lineage count has no upper bound, and expandLineages() materializes one entry per count, so a large count can exhaust memory before the pool cap applies.
fix: Enforce a max count and max expanded lineage total in parseFanoutConfig.

issue: Fanout concurrency is only required to be positive, not bounded, allowing a bad config to request an unbounded number of simultaneous lineage starts.
fix: Clamp or reject concurrency above a documented maximum.

issue: Fanout subprocesses use spawnSync with timeout but no detached process group cleanup, so CLI grandchildren can survive timeout or partial failure.
fix: Use the audited async supervisor or add detached process-group SIGTERM/SIGKILL cleanup.

issue: Each lineage receives the full parent process.env, bypassing executor-audit's env allowlist and dispatch-stack guard; this leaks unrelated provider secrets across executor kinds and weakens recursion protection.
fix: Build the child environment through buildExecutorDispatchEnv() and merge only lineage-specific state variables.

issue: Non-zero lineage subprocess exits are returned as normal worker output, so the pool counts them as fulfilled and downstream merge can skip missing registries and produce PASS from an empty review merge.
fix: Reject or mark failed on non-zero child exit, missing registry, or failed salvage, and make merge fail when any requested lineage lacks a valid registry.

issue: Fan-out skips phase_init and phase_main_loop, but synthesis still reads base state, strategy, and iteration files while merge only writes the base registry; successful lineage artifacts remain under lineages/{label}.
fix: Materialize base rollup state/strategy/iteration inputs during fanout merge, or add a fanout-specific synthesis path that reads lineage sub-packets directly.

issue: CLI fan-out uses spawnSync inside the async pool worker, so subprocess execution blocks the event loop and configured concurrency does not create true parallel lineage execution.
fix: Use async spawn, resolve on child close, and keep timeout/kill handling around the child process.
